subject: Re: Pro Mar Services, Inc. Survey
body:
asl lynn if the pilings were replaced  I don't think so.. if they wern't stick it up amerlins ass

----- Original Message ----
From: "DKIESQ@aol.com" <DKIESQ@aol.com>
To: jeeproject@yahoo.com
Sent: Tuesday, October 31, 2006 11:33:33 AM
Subject: Pro Mar Services, Inc. Survey



 

I spoke to Mark at Pro Mar Services who told me or confirmed the following 
relevant information:

 

1.    Pro Mar is not a marine surveyor; "its in the 
piling business"

 

2.    The tide information was for Benner Bay and 
Dohm's marina is in Jessup Bay; Mark claims that no tide information is 
available for Jessup bay.  Mark said there is very little tidal activity in 
the area anyway, so Mark believes the tidal info its probably not all that 
relevant.

 

3.    Mark claims that he took the readings at Zero 
tide, but when I asked him how he knows it was zero tide, he told me he would 
have to use tide scales.  When I reminded him that there is no tidal info 
for Jessup bay, he told me that there is probably no difference in height of 
tides between Benner and Jessup and only a matter of 30 minutes or so with 
respect to high and low tides.

 

4.    His physical survey was minimal, he simply looked 
at the dock.  He noted his belief the the light structure of the 
dock makes it suitable for light boats only and if you were to moor regular 
40 to 50 foot boats you would need a different structure.

 

5.    When I asked Mark how he knew what condition the 
pilings were in, he told me that the operator of the dock told him that pilings 
were replaced a few years back.  Moreover, Mark told me that the pilings 
are not driven into the sea bed; instead, the pilings are "Jetted in" with 
water.

 

6.    No evaluation of the floating office or 
the materials on the sea bed to which the floating office is moored.  
Mark's only comment was that the "office" was not much of anything and would be 
probably safer to get rid of it.

 

Conclusion: Rather than get a full survey of the marina as I asked him 
to, it appears John had one of his cronies drop depth lines to 
measure depth.and simply give a cursory look at the condition of the 
marina.  John knew that this was for a potential acquisition.

 

Darren K. 
Indyke
Attorney-At-Law
457 Madison Avenue
4th Floor
New York, New 
York 10022
Tel: 212-750-1176
Fax: 212-750-0381
e-mail: dkiesq@aol.com

asl lynn if the pilings were replaced  I don't think so.. if they wern't stick it up amerlins ass

----- Original Message ----
From: "DKIESQ@aol.com" <DKIESQ@aol.com>
To: jeeproject@yahoo.com
Sent: Tuesday, October 31, 2006 11:33:33 AM
Subject: Pro Mar Services, Inc. Survey

I spoke to Mark at Pro Mar Services who told me or confirmed the following relevant information:
 
1.    Pro Mar is not a marine surveyor; "its in the piling business"
 
2.    The tide information was for Benner Bay and Dohm's marina is in Jessup Bay; Mark claims that no tide information is available for Jessup bay.  Mark said there is very little tidal activity in the area anyway, so Mark believes the tidal info its probably not all that relevant.
 
3.    Mark claims that he took the readings at Zero tide, but when I asked him how he knows it was zero tide, he told me he would have to use tide scales.  When I reminded him that there is no tidal info for Jessup bay, he told me that there is probably no difference in height of tides between Benner and Jessup and only a matter of 30 minutes or so with respect to high and low tides.
 
4.    His physical survey was minimal, he simply looked at the dock.  He noted his belief the the light structure of the dock makes it suitable for light boats only and if you were to moor regular 40 to 50 foot boats you would need a different structure.
 
5.    When I asked Mark how he knew what condition the pilings were in, he told me that the operator of the dock told him that pilings were replaced a few years back.  Moreover, Mark told me that the pilings are not driven into the sea bed; instead, the pilings are "Jetted in" with water.
 
6.    No evaluation of the floating office or the materials on the sea bed to which the floating office is moored.  Mark's only comment was that the "office" was not much of anything and would be probably safer to get rid of it.
 
Conclusion: Rather than get a full survey of the marina as I asked him to, it appears John had one of his cronies drop depth lines to measure depth.and simply give a cursory look at the condition of the marina.  John knew that this was for a potential acquisition.
